research_mapping.claim_limit: This contract creates a development-only diagnosis mechanism. It makes no quality or held-out claim.

module_contract.role: Compile paired online-bin-packing order observations into regime-stratified FME feedback and counterexample metadata without changing the candidate score contract.

caller_obligations: Pass development-only observations for exactly one candidate and one frozen development suite.
Pass two valid order variants for every declared regime and multiset before requesting paired sensitivity.
Record capacity and hashes, but do not persist raw item sequences in the feedback artifact.
return_invariants: Every paired sensitivity compares identical multiset_hash values with distinct order_variant values.
The result contains no held-out identifier, future-item feature, provider secret, raw model response, or raw item sequence.

seam_rationale.module_depth: The Module hides regime classification, pair validation, sensitivity aggregation, ranking-flip detection, and archive-ready metadata behind one compile operation.
seam_rationale.leverage: The evaluator, feedback compiler, controller, and archive consume one summary instead of each reimplementing order pairing rules.
seam_rationale.locality: Future changes to thresholds, hashes, or pair checks remain in this Module rather than spreading through EOH prompts and archive callers.
seam_rationale.real_seam_condition: At implementation time, the existing aggregate v2 order summary is wrapped by aggregate_order_feedback_v2_compat and the new order_regime_feedback_v1 becomes a second Adapter that satisfies this Interface. No compatibility Adapter or production code is created by this contract.

required_fields.capacity: positive integer captured from the evaluated instance
required_fields.large_item_count: count of items satisfying item * 3 > capacity
required_fields.multiset_hash: SHA-256 of the sorted multiset and capacity
required_fields.order_hash: SHA-256 of the ordered sequence and capacity
required_fields.valid: evaluation completed without feasibility or timeout failure

required_fields.candidate_id: copied from the sole input candidate
required_fields.pair_order_sensitivity_pct: absolute random-versus-alternating gap for each regime:multiset pair
required_fields.large_item_order_sensitivity_pct: maximum paired sensitivity over large_dominant pairs
required_fields.worst_regime: regime with largest mean gap; lexical tie break
required_fields.worst_pair: regime:multiset with largest paired sensitivity; lexical tie break
required_fields.distinguishing_counterexample_ids: one worst valid order variant per regime plus an order-pair identifier
required_fields.behavior_profile_hash: canonical SHA-256 of the declared summary fields

optional_fields.ranking_flip_pairs: development-only records added only after two or more valid candidates share the same pair
optional_fields.invalid_observation_ids: invalid observations retained as explicit failure evidence

development_interval.capacity_source: per-instance evaluator capacity; the first BP implementation uses the existing capacity=100 development configuration without embedding 100 in the Adapter
development_interval.pair_rule: Both order variants are permutations of one sampled multiset, must have equal multiset_hash, and must have different order_hash values.
development_interval.large_item_rule: An item is large exactly when item * 3 > capacity. This is an evaluator descriptor, never a hidden candidate feature.

regimes.sampler: sample every item uniformly from 1 through floor(capacity / 3)
sample 1024 items uniformly from 1 through floor(capacity / 3) and 1024 items uniformly from floor(capacity / 3) + 1 through capacity, then deterministically permute the concatenated multiset
sample every item uniformly from floor(capacity / 3) + 1 through capacity

order_generation.random: the deterministic sampled permutation for the seed
order_generation.alternating_extremes: sort the same even-length multiset, then interleave the descending upper half with the ascending lower half

artifact_fields.instance_hash: SHA-256 of the ordered instance, capacity, regime_id, multiset_id, and order_variant

pair_admission_preconditions: Both variants are valid and share one multiset_hash.
The declared regime matches the measured large_item_fraction classifier.
The order_hash values differ; otherwise the pair is rejected as non-discriminating.
No observation or artifact refers to a held-out suite.
counterexample_contract.archive_admission_rule: Call the existing FMEArchives.admit_counterexample without weakening its four frozen checks: no duplicate counterexample ID; at least one valid candidate; a globally strong candidate is worse than the best candidate on that counterexample by more than 1e-12; and the artifact adds a new feature region or candidate ranking signature.
counterexample_contract.ranking_flip_rule: For one regime:multiset pair, record a ranking flip only when at least two valid candidate IDs have strict opposite pairwise order between random and alternating_extremes, using 1e-12 as the tie tolerance. A flip is a controller cue for compare_on_counterexample, not an admission substitute and not a quality claim.
counterexample_contract.duplicate_policy: If a pair adds neither a new feature_region nor a new ranking_signature, retain the observation in the development record but do not admit a new counterexample artifact.

future_implementation_files: eoh_rag/fme/recorder.py (consume the existing archive rule without relaxing it)
official_eoh/eoh/src/eoh/eoh/eoh.py (surface only the summary fields needed by FMEControllerState)
integration_plan.controller_mapping: A non-empty ranking_flip_pairs field may increment pending_counterexample_comparisons and cause the existing single-action controller to choose compare_on_counterexample. It must not directly call an EOH operator, modify a candidate, or authorize a transfer.
implementation_preconditions: Implement the new Module before adding a runtime profile reference.
Preserve the current candidate score contract and all FME v2 profile names.
Perform only static inspection or approved targeted verification after code changes; do not run tests automatically.

isolation_rules: Do not modify the v2 manifest, its arms, seed lists, gates, profiles, or held-out fields.
Do not add this Adapter as a v2 arm, metric, prompt field, or post-hoc analysis rule.
Do not generate or inspect fme_unseen_v1 or fme_unseen_v2 through this Module.
A future order_regime cohort, if ever proposed, must use a separately frozen manifest, fresh development seeds, separate cohort ID, and newly approved LLM budget.
No current v2 result, candidate, or held-out observation may be reclassified using this contract.

reject_before_any_llm_cohort_if: The implementation cannot prove each pair has matching multiset_hash values and distinct order_hash values.
Any regime fails its declared large_item_fraction classifier or reuses a prohibited seed range.
The adapter requires raw future information, a candidate signature change, a held-out value, or a hard-coded capacity constant.
A development-only comparison of at least two pre-existing distinct candidate code hashes yields no distinct behavior summary and no new ranking signature.
All otherwise valid pairs duplicate existing feature regions and ranking signatures.
halt_and_audit_if: The frozen v2 manifest hash changes before a separately authorized baseline revision.
A proposed implementation attempts to run a cohort, call an LLM provider, or expose held-out data without explicit budget and launch authorization.
exit_conditions.success_condition_for_optional_retention: The offline development-only implementation demonstrates a valid regime-separated summary for at least two existing distinct candidates and yields at least one archive-admissible or ranking-flip counterexample under the unchanged archive rule. This permits a separate proposal only; it is not permission to run one.

evidence_refs.purpose: current BP development evaluator and order-pair generation path
